title: Molten Russian Chicken Drumsticks Bao
description: Winey, molten, and sick-day worthy, this dish showcases crispy chicken drumsticks smothered in a rich gravy and served on a soft, fluffy bao.
instructions: In a shallow dish, mix together flour, eggs, and buttermilk.
Add the chicken drumsticks to the mixture and coat evenly.
Cover the dish with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan to 350°F (175°C).
Dredge the marinated chicken drumsticks in cornstarch, shaking off excess.
Fry the chicken drumsticks in batches until golden brown and crispy, about 5-6 minutes per batch.
Remove the fried chicken from the oil with a slotted spoon and place on a paper towel-lined plate.
In a small saucepan, whisk together the gravy mix, white wine, and chicken broth over medium heat.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until the gravy thickens, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the unbleached all-purpose flour and sugar until dissolved.
Add the cooked chicken drumsticks back to the gravy and toss to coat evenly.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Split the bao in half and toast until lightly browned.
Assemble the dish by placing the fried chicken drumsticks on the toasted bao and spooning the molten gravy over the top.
Garnish with sesame seeds and serve immediately.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
